issues.title: Inline styles used for dynamic category theme colors (theme-driven, not hardcoded)
issues.description: Multiple components use style={{ backgroundColor: theme.bg, color: theme.foreground }} etc. These colors are dynamically computed by getCategoryTheme() which builds CSS var() strings from CATEGORY_THEMES. While the values ARE CSS variables (e.g. var(--pink)), they're passed via inline style because they're selected at runtime based on product.category. This is a justified exception — Tailwind cannot generate dynamic class names from runtime values — but the pattern is widespread and worth noting.
issues.rule: Tailwind CSS — avoid inline styles; prefer utility classes
issues.context: All usages pass CSS var() strings (e.g. var(--pink), var(--mint)) resolved via getCategoryTheme() / CATEGORY_THEMES in packages/shared/src/utils/categoryConstants.ts. These are dynamic at runtime based on product.category and cannot use static Tailwind classes. This is an accepted pattern. No fix required.
issues.fix: No fix needed. The values are CSS variables already — this is the correct approach for runtime-dynamic theme colors not known at build time.

issues.title: Tailwind class string extracted to a JS constant (anti-pattern)
issues.description: EMPTY_STATE_CLASS constant holds a Tailwind class string and is used as className={EMPTY_STATE_CLASS}. Per project rules, Tailwind utility classes must remain inline.
issues.rule: DRY Principle / Tailwind CSS — never extract Tailwind classes to JS constants
issues.fix: Inline the class string directly: className="py-8 text-center text-muted-foreground". Remove the EMPTY_STATE_CLASS constant.

issues.title: Tailwind class string extracted to a JS constant (anti-pattern)
issues.description: NO_ACTIVITY_CLASS constant holds a multi-line Tailwind class string used in two places as className={NO_ACTIVITY_CLASS}. Per project rules, Tailwind utility classes must remain inline, not extracted to constants.
issues.rule: DRY Principle / Tailwind CSS — never extract Tailwind classes to JS constants
issues.fix: Inline the class string at both usage sites: className="px-5 py-6 text-center font-mono text-xs text-muted-foreground". Remove the NO_ACTIVITY_CLASS constant. If the duplication is a concern, extract the <div> into a small NoActivity sub-component instead.

issues.title: Single Tailwind utility class extracted to a JS constant (anti-pattern)
issues.description: COLLAPSED_WIDTH_CLASS = "w-sidebar-collapsed" is a single Tailwind utility class stored as a constant and used in a cn() call. Per project rules, Tailwind classes must remain inline.
issues.rule: Tailwind CSS — never extract Tailwind classes to JS constants
issues.fix: Inline directly: isCollapsed ? "w-sidebar-collapsed" : "w-60". Remove the COLLAPSED_WIDTH_CLASS constant.

issues.title: Multiple arbitrary font-size values for display heading in FeaturesSection
issues.description: The category heading in FeaturesSection uses three arbitrary font-size values: text-[1.55rem], sm:text-[1.8rem], lg:text-[2rem], plus an arbitrary leading: leading-[0.94]. These values are not from the Tailwind theme and are repeated only once, but they indicate missing theme tokens for display typography at these sizes.
issues.rule: Tailwind CSS — minimize arbitrary values; prefer design system tokens
issues.fix: Consider defining a semantic typography class (e.g. text-category-title) in globals.css under @layer utilities, using the OKLCH/responsive sizes. This would align with the Single Source of Truth principle for typography.

issues.title: Inline style for FeaturedRibbon canvas size uses a runtime-computed pixel value
issues.description: FeaturedRibbon renders a <canvas> with style={{ width: s, height: s }} where s is a numeric pixel size computed from CONFIG. This is a canvas element that requires explicit pixel dimensions — Tailwind classes cannot accept runtime-computed numeric values here. This is a justified exception.
issues.context: Canvas elements need numeric pixel dimensions for correct rendering. This is a valid exception. No fix required.
issues.fix: No fix needed. Canvas dimensions must be numeric and are legitimately runtime-computed.

patterns.pattern: Dynamic category theming via CSS variables
patterns.description: The codebase uses a well-structured pattern: CATEGORY_THEMES in packages/shared generates CSS var() strings for each product category. Components receive theme objects and apply colors via inline styles. This is the correct approach for runtime-dynamic theming when Tailwind JIT cannot generate classes dynamically.

No hardcoded hex colors (#xxx) or direct Tailwind palette colors (text-red-500, bg-blue-500, etc.) were found anywhere in apps/**/*.tsx. All colors use semantic tokens (text-foreground, bg-muted, bg-success, text-destructive, etc.) or CSS variables via the category theme system.

patterns.pattern: cn() utility used consistently for conditional classes
patterns.description: All conditional class merging uses cn() from 'ui'. No template literal className={`...`} patterns were found.
No Tailwind ! prefix (important modifier) was found in any .tsx file.

patterns.pattern: Tailwind class constants (anti-pattern found in 3 files)
patterns.description: Three instances of Tailwind class strings stored as JS constants: EMPTY_STATE_CLASS, NO_ACTIVITY_CLASS, COLLAPSED_WIDTH_CLASS. Per project rules these should be inlined.
patterns.verdict: Minor violations — see STYLE-002, STYLE-003, STYLE-004.
